words.hint: A round object used in many games.
Moving through water on purpose.
The player who tries to stop shots from entering the net.
The person who enforces the rules during a match.
A long-distance running event of about 42 kilometres.
A free attempt awarded for a rule violation by the other side.
A winter event that combines cross-country skiing with shooting.
A track-and-field competition with ten different events.
An ice sport using brooms and heavy round stones.
A multi-event competition originating in the ancient games, featuring five disciplines.
A tall plant with a trunk and leaves.
Water falling from the sky.
A long body of fresh water that flows toward the sea.
A large area densely covered with trees.
A huge mass of ice that moves slowly across land.
A mountain that occasionally erupts molten rock.
A seasonal wind that brings heavy rains, especially across South Asia.
The place where a river meets the sea, mixing fresh and salt water.
A treeless biome where the ground stays frozen below the surface.
A grassy plain with scattered trees, typical of tropical regions.
A device used to talk to people who are far away.
What you press to enter letters into a computer.
Software you use to visit websites.
The technology that lets devices connect to the internet without cables.
Scrambling information so only intended readers can understand it.
An organised collection of information that programs can query.
A tool that turns human-written source into machine instructions.
A network barrier that allows or blocks traffic by rules.
Relating to the smallest discrete units of physical reality.
A shared, append-only ledger replicated across many computers.
A baked staple made from flour, water and yeast.
Solid dairy product made by curdling milk.
A thin, round batter cooked on a flat pan.
A long strand of dough boiled in water.
Cooking meat outdoors over open heat or coals.
A small parcel of dough wrapped around a filling.
A small, concentrated coffee shot pulled under pressure.
A flaky, buttery, crescent-shaped pastry.
A Provençal stew of summer vegetables — eggplant, zucchini, tomato.
Thinly sliced raw fish served without rice.
A small furry pet that purrs and meows.
A large hoofed animal often ridden by people.
A flightless bird that waddles on ice.
A sea creature with eight arms and remarkable intelligence.
An Australian marsupial that hops on powerful legs.
A lizard famous for changing its colour.
An Arctic whale with a long, spiraled tusk.
A scaly mammal that curls into a ball when threatened.
A Mexican salamander that keeps its larval features for life.
An egg-laying mammal with a duck-like bill and a beaver-like tail.
A drawing that shows where places are.
A small paper or code that lets you board a vehicle.
An official booklet that proves your nationality at borders.
A rectangular bag with wheels you take on a trip.
A small object you bring home to remember a place.
A planned schedule of where to go and when.
A break at an airport between two connecting flights.
A country's official mission inside another country's capital.
A stamp or sticker that authorises you to enter another country.
The fatigue from rapidly crossing several time zones.
A percussion instrument you hit with sticks or hands.
A string instrument you strum or pluck.
The repeating section of a song between verses.
The pattern of beats that makes you nod your head.
A four-string instrument played with a bow.
Notes sounding together that please the ear.
A curved brass wind instrument famous in jazz.
A device that ticks at a steady tempo for practice.
Playing the notes of a chord one after another instead of together.
Music with two or more independent melodic lines at once.
Someone who pretends to be a character on screen.
The big flat surface where a film is projected.
A short preview that advertises an upcoming film.
The salty puffed snack everyone eats at the cinema.
The person who gives the cast and crew their creative orders.
A brief, surprise appearance by a famous person in a film.

All the music and audio that plays during a film.
A scripted film shot as if it were a documentary.
A director whose distinctive vision dominates every film they make.
